transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-07-28]
[00:00] You: Incident postmortem — the database outage on July 25th. Let's agree on remediation deadlines.
[00:50] Speaker 1: The root cause was a missing connection pool timeout configuration. I'll deploy the fix to production by July 30th — two days from now.
[01:25] Speaker 2: The monitoring gap that prevented us from catching this earlier: I'll add the connection pool saturation alert to Datadog by July 31st.
[02:00] Speaker 3: We should also add a circuit breaker to the API gateway so a database saturation event doesn't take down the entire API. I'll implement that by August 7th.
[02:45] You: What about the affected customers?
[03:00] Speaker 1: I sent initial apology emails within 4 hours of the outage. I'll send the full postmortem summary to affected customers by end of next week, August 7th.
[03:35] Speaker 4: There were 3 enterprise customers who had SLA breaches. I'll reach out to each of them today to acknowledge and offer SLA credits. The SLA credit process takes 2 weeks, so credits will be processed by August 11th.
[04:15] Speaker 2: We should also review our runbook — it took 23 minutes to identify the root cause during the incident. I'll update the database incident runbook to include the connection pool checks by August 5th.
[04:50] Speaker 3: And we need a load test that simulates connection pool saturation so we can verify the fix. I'll set up and run that test by August 10th.
[05:20] Speaker 1: The all-clear to development teams on the root cause and prevention steps — I'll send that internal summary today.
[05:40] You: Good. Follow-up review in 30 days to verify all remediations are in place — so around August 28th.
